soap:
RE-REVIEW · lance-format/lance#6890 — feat: add early termination for compaction plan with max_compaction_bytes option
reviewed 7d3c363d → ea8093f4  (4 commits, 1 file in scope: rust/lance/src/dataset/optimize.rs)
note: 1 file excluded as base-branch merge churn, not the PR's own work.

SUBJECTIVE
The author pushed 4 commits since my last review. Their resolved-thread claim is
that they addressed the duplication suggestion. The diff also carries two changes
I did not previously ask for: a usize→u64 retype of all byte accounting, and a
rewrite of the bytes-budget test.

OBJECTIVE (verified against the ea8093f4 patch and full file, not GitHub flags)
1. Duplication removed. The "if !is_noop { accumulate frags + inline byte-sum };
   push bin; check budget" block that previously appeared verbatim in two match
   arms (the `else` re-binning arm and the `(None, Some(_))` flush arm) is now a
   single method `DefaultCompactionPlanner::finalize_bin` (optimize.rs:636),
   called at both sites (optimize.rs:771, :793). The inline
   `flat_map(files).filter_map(file_size_bytes).map(get).sum` byte-summing that
   recurred three times is centralized into `CandidateBin::fragment_known_bytes`
   (optimize.rs:1093) plus a cached per-bin `known_bytes` field (optimize.rs:1089).
   Verified one remaining inline sum at optimize.rs:661, but that is in
   `apply_budget_limits` over `TaskData` (a different type, post-binning) — a
   legitimately separate use, not the duplicated pattern.
2. Byte accounting retyped usize→u64 across `exceeds_budget`, the running totals,
   `apply_budget_limits`, `max_compaction_bytes`, and `known_bytes`. No residual
   `as usize` casts on byte values remain; budget math no longer depends on
   pointer width (correct on 32-bit targets).
3. Incremental `known_bytes` bookkeeping is balanced: `+= new_fragment_bytes` on
   add (optimize.rs:754) is mirrored by `-= new_fragment_bytes` on the
   budget-revert pop (optimize.rs:764). `split_for_size` resets split-off bins to
   `known_bytes: 0` (optimize.rs:1131), which is safe because splitting runs after
   budget enforcement and never re-reads the field for budget purposes.
4. Test rewrite: the degenerate `max_compaction_bytes: Some(1)` boundary is
   replaced by computing `first_task_bytes` from the unlimited plan and asserting
   both `limited_frags > 0` and `limited_frags < unlimited_frags` — a meaningful
   budget assertion rather than a trivially-zero edge.

ASSESSMENT
The single open condition is MET, and met by real code, not by the resolved-thread
claim. The fresh-eyes pass over everything that changed since 7d3c363 surfaced no
new correctness, overflow, or symmetry defects: the u64 migration is complete and
consistent, the cached-bytes invariant holds on all add/revert/split paths, and
the test is strengthened rather than weakened. No new blocking concerns.

CONDITIONS LEDGER (1/1 met)
  ~ MET  [suggestion] "this pattern appears three times — extract into a function
         or a method on a bin?"
         → extracted to finalize_bin (optimize.rs:636, called :771/:793) and
           fragment_known_bytes (optimize.rs:1093); was author-marked resolved AND
           confirmed in the diff.

FRESH-EYES DELTA
  No new concerns. The unrequested u64 retype and test rewrite were reviewed
  independently and are both improvements; the new `known_bytes` cache is
  invariant-correct across add, budget-revert, and split paths.

PLAN / RECOMMENDATION: approve
  confidence: high · what would make this wrong: if `known_bytes` were consumed by
  a budget check on a post-split bin (it is not — split is size-only and runs after
  budget), or if a 32-bit overflow path I did not enumerate still used `usize` (the
  full-file grep for `as usize` on byte values came back clean).

drafted blocking comments: 0
